fullDisplayName: Single Strike Style Mustard TG28/TG30 Brilliant Stars
rules:
You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.

Search your deck for a Single Strike Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. If you searched your deck in this way, draw 5 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

fullDisplayName: Rapid Strike Style Mustard TG27/TG30 Brilliant Stars
rules:
You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.

Put a Rapid Strike Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench. If you do, draw 5 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Choose up to 3 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a different type of basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Your opponent reveals their hand, and you draw a card for each Trainer card you find there.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.
VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.effect: Attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal 120 damage from that Pokémon.

attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.
V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Electrify
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Bolt Storm
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Lightning Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: It knows the forest inside and out. If it comes across a wounded Pokémon, Oranguru will gather medicinal herbs to treat it.

attacks.name: Vee-Search
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon V, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.
Identifiable by its eerie howls, people a long time ago thought it was the grim reaper and feared it.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It spews threads from its mouth to catch its prey. When night falls, it leaves its web to go hunt aggressively.

attacks.name: Rainbow Flavor
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each type of basic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: When Alcremie is content, the cream it secretes from its hands becomes sweeter and richer.

attacks.name: Dede-Flash
attacks.effect: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 60 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Since Dedenne can't generate as much electricity on its own, it steals electricity from outlets or other electric Pokémon.
At the bidding of transmissions from the spirit world, it steals people and Pokémon away. No one knows whether it has a will of its own.

attacks.name: Wild Shock
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When the interior part of its tail spins like a motor, Zekrom can generate many bolts of lightning to blast its surroundings.
If it is angered or startled, the fur all over its body bristles like sharp needles that pierce foes.
It traps enemies with its suction-cupped tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.
When Vaporeon's fins begin to vibrate, it is a sign that rain will come within a few hours.
Once it has stored up enough heat, this Pokémon's body temperature can reach up to 1,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

rules:

As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides ColorlessColorless Energy. The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 less damage to your opponent's Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand. Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Shuffle up to 3 in any combination of Pokémon and Supporter cards, except for Team Yell's Cheer, from your discard pile into your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Choose 1 or more:
• Shuffle a Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.
• Shuffle a Pokémon Tool card from your discard pile into your deck.
• Shuffle a Stadium card from your discard pile into your deck.
• Shuffle an Energy card from your discard pile into your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

If the Pokémon this card is attached to doesn't have a Rule Box, it takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Once during each player's turn, that player may attach a Fire Energy card from their discard pile to 1 of their Benched Fire Pokémon. If a player attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

You can use this card only if you discard a Fire Energy card from your hand. Look at the top 7 cards of your deck and put up to 2 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Dragon Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon that don't have a Rule Box and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn, draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand instead.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Each player can't have more than 4 Benched Pokémon. If a player has 5 or more Benched Pokémon, they discard Benched Pokémon until they have 4 Pokémon on the Bench. The player who played this card discards first. If more than one effect changes the number of Benched Pokémon allowed, use the smaller number.

The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Psychic Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon V (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Put 1 of your Colorless Pokémon that has any damage counters on it and all attached cards into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Choose up to 3 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a different type of basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

If the Pokémon this card is attached to uses an attack, if you flip any coins for the damage or effect of that attack, and if any of them are tails, draw 3 cards at the end of your turn.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Your opponent reveals their hand, and you draw a card for each Trainer card you find there.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.effect: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Showboating Pose
attacks.effect: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It drives its opponents to exhaustion with its agile maneuvers, then ends the fight with a flashy finishing move.
Tornadus expels massive energy from its tail, causing severe storms. Its power is great enough to blow houses away.

attacks.name: Triple Axel
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won't tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

attacks.name: Trinity Nova
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon V in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Trinity Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon V in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Tail Smash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. It is known as an industrious worker.
It constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle down its front teeth. It nests alongside water.

attacks.name: Strong Breeze
attacks.effect: Your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck.

attacks.name: Spinning Bird
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.
It lives in forests and fields. Squabbles over territory occur when flocks collide.

attacks.name: Claw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Hurricane
attacks.effect: Move a basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its form changes depending on the weather. The rougher conditions get, the rougher Castform's disposition!

attacks.name: Leek Lash
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The stalk this Pokémon carries in its wings serves as a sword to cut down opponents. In a dire situation, the stalk can also serve as food.

attacks.name: Slosh 'n' Crash
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon. If you discarded a Pokémon Tool in this way, this attack does 120 more damage.

attacks.name: Dragon Strike
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Dragon Strike.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Revenge
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon during their last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: Druddigon are vicious and cunning. They take up residence in nests dug out by other Pokémon, treating the stolen nests as their own lairs.

attacks.name: Wild Axe
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its resilient tusks are its pride and joy. It licks up dirt to take in the minerals it needs to keep its tusks in top condition.
After battle, this Pokémon carefully sharpens its tusks on river rocks. It needs to take care of its tusks—if one breaks, it will never grow back.

attacks.name: Ultra Evolution
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Haxorus and put it onto this Axew to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: These Pokémon nest in the ground and use their tusks to crush hard berries. Crushing berries is also how they test each other's strength.

attacks.name: Dragonblade
attacks.effect: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

flavorText: Garchomp makes its home in volcanic mountains. It flies through the sky as fast as a jet airplane, hunting down as much prey as it can.
This Pokémon emits ultrasonic waves from a protrusion on either side of its head to probe pitch-dark caves.
Gible prefers to stay in narrow holes in the sides of caves heated by geothermal energy. This way, Gible can stay warm even during a blizzard.

attacks.name: Draconic Impulse
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, this attack does 160 more damage, and discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Revenge Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Tumbling Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: From its spikes, it launches powerful blasts of electricity. Its red core contains an enormous amount of energy.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: When Klang goes all out, the minigear links up perfectly with the outer part of the big gear, and this Pokémon's rotation speed increases sharply.
The two minigears that compose this Pokémon are closer than twins. They mesh well only with each other.

attacks.name: Pike
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: They use shells they've stolen from Shelmet to arm and protect themselves. They're very popular Pokémon in the Galar region.

attacks.name: Guard Claw
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Iron Hammer
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.

attacks.name: Magnetic Tension

attacks.name: Iron Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It uses three small units to catch prey and battle enemies. The main body mostly just gives orders.

attacks.name: Matron's Anger
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.

flavorText: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon's body. The cloak is never shed.

attacks.name: Cracking Stomp
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Max Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 150 more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gnaw and Run
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hangry Spike
attacks.effect: If you played Marnie's Pride from your hand during this turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Longhair Shot
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Darkness Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: With the hair wrapped around its body helping to enhance its muscles, this Pokémon can overwhelm even Machamp.
When it gets down on all fours as if to beg for forgiveness, it's trying to lure opponents in so that it can stab them with its spear-like hair.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Through its nose, it sucks in the emanations produced by people and Pokémon when they feel annoyed. It thrives off this negative energy.
Don't be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It steals things from people just to amuse itself with their frustration. A rivalry exists between this Pokémon and Nickit.

attacks.name: Ticking Terror
attacks.effect: Until the end of your next turn, the Defending Pokémon's Weakness is now Darkness. (The amount of Weakness doesn't change.)

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.effect: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Exactly 108 spirits gathered to become this Pokémon. Apparently there are some ill-natured spirits in the mix.

attacks.name: Fearsome Shadow
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ransack
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is heads, your opponent reveals their hand. For each heads, choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of your opponent's deck in any order.

flavorText: They attack their quarry in packs. Prey as large as Mamoswine easily fall to the teamwork of a group of Weavile.
Its paws conceal sharp claws. If attacked, it suddenly extends the claws and startles its enemy.

attacks.name: Shrieking Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavorText: Because they scatter germs everywhere, they've long been targeted for extermination, leading to a steep decline in their population.

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The wastewater coming from factories is clean these days, so Grimer have nothing to eat. They're said to be on the verge of extinction.

attacks.name: Big Hand
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each card in your hand.

flavorText: Artillery platforms built into the walls of ancient castles served as perches from which Golurk could fire energy beams.
They were sculpted from clay in ancient times. No one knows why, but some of them are driven to continually line up boulders.

attacks.name: Sweep the Leg
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: If you see a Sawk training in the mountains in its single-minded pursuit of strength, it's best to quietly pass by.
It performs throwing moves with first-rate skill. Over the course of many battles, Throh's belt grows darker as it absorbs its wearer's sweat.

attacks.name: Aura Sphere Volley
attacks.effect: Discard all Fighting Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 60 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It controls waves known as auras, which are powerful enough to pulverize huge rocks. It uses these waves to take down its prey.
It's exceedingly energetic, with enough stamina to keep running all through the night. Taking it for walks can be a challenging experience.

attacks.name: Matron's Anger
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon's body. The cloak is never shed.

attacks.name: Desert Pillar
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: This Pokémon hides in the heart of sandstorms it creates and seldom appears where people can see it.
The ultrasonic waves it generates by rubbing its two wings together cause severe headaches.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Its nest is a sloped, bowl-like pit in the desert. Once something has fallen in, there is no escape.
It hunts without twitching a muscle by pulling in its prey with powerful magnetism. But sometimes it pulls natural enemies in close.

attacks.name: Spinning Draw
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: It launches kicks while spinning. If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.

attacks.name: Rainbow Flavor
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each type of basic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: When Alcremie is content, the cream it secretes from its hands becomes sweeter and richer.

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: They say that any patisserie visited by Milcery is guaranteed success and good fortune.

attacks.name: Ominous Numbers
attacks.effect: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like. If you played Acerola's Premonition from your hand during this turn, place 13 damage counters instead.

attacks.name: Max Shadow
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Jealous Eyes
attacks.effect: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dede-Flash
attacks.effect: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 60 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Since Dedenne can't generate as much electricity on its own, it steals electricity from outlets or other electric Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tri Recharge
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. Attach a number of basic Energy cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Psychic power allows these Pokémon to fly. Some say they were the guardians of an ancient city. Others say they were the guardians' emissaries.

attacks.name: Trick Wind
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Pokémon Tool or Special Energy cards from their hand.

attacks.name: Fluffball Star
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fluff Gets in the Way
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Cotton Guard
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Clear Tone
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Special Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Emitting ultrasonic cries, it floats on winds to travel great distances.
At the bidding of transmissions from the spirit world, it steals people and Pokémon away. No one knows whether it has a will of its own.

attacks.name: Fade to Black
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its body is entirely hollow. When it opens its mouth, it sucks everything in as if it were a black hole.

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: If it finds bad children who won't listen to their parents, it will spirit them away—or so it's said.

attacks.name: Coinciding Figures
attacks.effect: If you and your opponent have the same number of Benched Pokémon, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: This mysterious Pokémon started life as an ancient clay figurine made over 20,000 years ago.

attacks.name: Triple Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It moves while spinning around on its single foot. Some Baltoy have been seen spinning on their heads.

attacks.name: Chomp
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bull Dash
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Life Sucker
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew's genes. It's said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon has an organ known as its core. The organ glows in seven colors when Starmie is unleashing its potent psychic powers.

attacks.name: Moonlit Miracle
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. Choose a number of your Pokémon in play up to the number of heads. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.
It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

attacks.name: Windup Thunder
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flash Impact
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Luxray can see through solid objects. It will instantly spot prey trying to hide behind walls, even if the walls are thick.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: By joining its tail with that of another Luxio, this Pokémon can receive some of the other Luxio's electricity and power up its own electric blasts.
This Pokémon generates electricity by contracting its muscles. Excited trembling is a sign that Shinx is generating a tremendous amount of electricity.

attacks.name: Lightning Rondo
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Explosive Bolt
attacks.effect: If any of your Benched Magmortar have any damage counters on them, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: High-Voltage Current
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The amount of electrical energy this Pokémon produces is proportional to the rate of its pulse. The voltage jumps while Electivire is battling.

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

attacks.name: Fast Charge
attacks.effect: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Dynamic Spark
attacks.effect: You may discard any amount of Lightning Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Block Slider
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Fusion Strike Energy attached to all of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The hair on its head connects to the surface of its brain. When this Pokémon has something on its mind, its hair chills the air around it.

attacks.name: Sheer Cold
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It swims through frigid seas, searching for prey. From its frozen breath, it forms icy fangs that are harder than steel.
When this Pokémon is in good health, its snot becomes thicker and stickier. It will smear its snot on anyone it doesn't like.
It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aqua Return
attacks.effect: Shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Floatify
attacks.effect: Put up to 2 Item cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Its flotation sac developed as a result of pursuing aquatic prey. It can double as a rubber raft.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It inflates the flotation sac around its neck and pokes its head out of the water to see what is going on.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The three horns that extend from its beak attest to its power. The leader has the biggest horns.
It lives a solitary life. Its wings deliver wicked blows that can snap even the thickest of trees.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

attacks.name: Ice Breath
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It can only survive in cold areas. It bounces happily around, even in environments as cold as -150 degrees Fahrenheit.

attacks.name: Crab Impact
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: A rough customer that wildly flails its giant claws. It is said to be extremely hard to raise.
No matter how dirty the water in the river, it will adapt and thrive. It has a strong will to survive.

attacks.name: Raging Freeze
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon during their last turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: If you visit a beach at the end of summer, you'll be able to see groups of Staryu lighting up in a steady rhythm.

attacks.name: Bubbles Galore
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 5 Water Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: G-Max Pincer
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Falling Bubbles
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 5 Water Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Raging Pincer
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bursting Power
attacks.effect: You may attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Flare Juggling
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Infernal Vortex
attacks.effect: Reveal the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 80 damage for each Energy card you find there. Then, discard those Energy cards and shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

attacks.name: Burning Kick
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It tosses its enemies around with agility. It uses all its limbs to fight in its own unique style.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It uses ceilings and walls to launch aerial attacks. Its fiery tail is but one weapon.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The gas made in its belly burns from its rear end. The fire burns weakly when it feels sick.

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows out black soot if it is endangered.

attacks.name: Burning Rondo
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Inferno Wings
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 70 more damage. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

flavorText: It's one of the legendary bird Pokémon. When Moltres flaps its flaming wings, they glimmer with a dazzling red glow.

attacks.name: Boltsplosion
attacks.effect: If Electivire is on your Bench, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: When Magmortar inhales deeply, the fire burning in its belly intensifies, rising in temperature to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.
Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

attacks.name: Explosive Fire
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.

attacks.name: Star Blaze
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Incinerate
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Leap to Leap
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Jungle Rage
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 120 more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Its strange physiology reacts to electrical energy in interesting ways. The presence of a Shelmet will cause this Pokémon to evolve.

attacks.name: Revenge Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

rules: VSTAR rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Revenge Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: The deeper a Cherubi's red, the more nutrients it has stockpiled in its body. And the sweeter and tastier its small ball!

attacks.name: Raid
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon evolved from Burmy during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It flutters around at night and steals honey from the Combee hive.

attacks.name: Matron's Anger
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.

attacks.name: Leaf Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon's body. The cloak is never shed.
If its cloak is broken in battle, it quickly remakes the cloak with materials nearby.

attacks.name: Evopress
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Evolution Pokémon in play.

flavorText: Small Pokémon occasionally gather on its unmoving back to begin building their nests.
It knows where pure water wells up. It carries fellow Pokémon there on its back.
It undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.
Bunches of delicious fruit grow around its neck. In warm areas, many ranches raise Tropius.

attacks.name: Spore Ball
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Powdery Uppercut
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if this Pokémon used Spore Ball during your last turn.

flavorText: It scatters poisonous spores and throws powerful punches while its foe is hampered by inhaled spores.
It spouts poison spores from the top of its head. These spores cause pain all over if inhaled.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Each of Exeggutor's three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don't seem to be very interested in one another.
Though it may look like it's just a bunch of eggs, it's a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.
